pattern: Counting frequencies then sorting by descending count with lexicographic tie-breaking yields a fully determined ranking, of which the first k entries are taken.
hints: First tally how many times each distinct word appears.
The required order is by count from high to low, breaking ties alphabetically, so a single sort with a composite key suffices.
Build a frequency map, sort the unique words by (-count, word), and return the first k words.

edge_inputs.description: k smaller than number of equal-frequency words picks alphabetically first
k equals number of unique words
longer words with shared prefixes sort by full string

stress.description: many lowercase words from a small alphabet with a valid random k
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 500))
    import string
    pool = []
    for _ in range(60):
        ln = rng.randrange(1, 11)
        pool.append(''.join(rng.choice(string.ascii_lowercase) for _ in range(ln)))
    words = [rng.choice(pool) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randrange(1, len(set(words)) + 1)
    return [words, k]
stress.note: k always within [1, number of unique words]
